title: Dynamic Validation
text: Dynamic Validation Procedural validation is not necessarily fixed at compile time. By using the $condition and $expression functions, it is possible to change complex mathematical validation rules in a deployed application. These functions interpret mathematical expressions at run time. The expressions can be entered by a user or read from a database. When using $condition and $expression , consider the following: These functions refer directly to application objects such as field and variable names, as well as ProcScript functions. If the validation rules are to be administered directly by non-technical users, some effort will have to be expended hiding this internal complexity. Testing an application with dynamic validation rules can be substantially more complicated than testing an application with static validation. Related Links $condition Return the result of evaluating a conditional expression. $expression Return the result of evaluating a nonconditional expression. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
